title: agalinis paupercula
intro: Agalinis paupercula, also known as small whorled pogonia, is a perennial plant species that belongs to the Orobanchaceae family. It is a parasitic plant that obtains its nutrients by attaching itself to the roots of other plants, typically grasses and wildflowers. The medicinal properties of Agalinis paupercula include its ability to act as a diuretic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant, which are often used to treat conditions such as urinary issues, skin irritations, and oxidative stress. Herbal preparations of Agalinis paupercula can be made into teas, tinctures, or infusions, and have been traditionally used to promote digestion, relieve menstrual cramps, and support immune function.
